channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Lagos: Two arraigned over alleged N63.7m shipping container fraud

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya makes history as NBA’s second female President in 34 years
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Badejo-Okusanya will serve a two-year tenure from 2026 to 2028, succeeding Afam Osigwe, SAN. Her election marks a significant milestone for the association, making her the first woman to lead the NBA in more than three decades after Mrs. Priscilla Kuye, who served as president between 1991 and 1992.

item.title: Osun 2026: Parties sign peace accord ahead of election
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: With less than a month to the poll and the tempo of election-related violence rising, participants stressed the need for restraint and education of all on the imperatives of peace and security.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ugondo Mdema Development Association (UMDA) has raised the alarm over an alleged planned attack on Tor-Damisa community in Donga Local Government Area of Taraba State by suspected Fulani militia. item.title: “Jarvis Complements Me” – Peller

item.description: Nigerian TikTok creator Peller has said he is eager to marry his fiancée, Jarvis, describing her as someone who complements him. Speaking during a conversation with Cubana Chief Priest, Peller said he believes delaying marriage is unnecessary, adding that if he and Jarvis were to separate, it would

item.title: Access Holdings sells 7.44% stake in Ghana unit
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Access Holdings Plc has completed the sale of a 7.44% stake in its Ghanaian subsidiary, a move analysts say is closely tied to a Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) rule capping how much local banks can hold in foreign units. The post Access Holdings sells 7.44% stake in Ghana unit appeared first on Naira

item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: One of the suspects admitted that he was to receive ₦10,000 as commission on each child every month, while ₦20,000 would be paid as the child's monthly wage. The post Police Rescue Nine Children, Arrest Two Suspected Human Traffickers In Niger appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Troops foil mass abduction attempt at FGGC Monguno
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The FGGC facility is currently being utilized by the Borno State Government as temporary hostel accommodation for students of the Federal Polytechnic, Monguno town. The post Troops foil mass abduction attempt at FGGC Monguno appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: FIFA World Cup final: 5 things to expect from Argentina vs Spain
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: The stage is set for a blockbuster as the 2026 FIFA World Cup comes to an end, with Spain and Argentina preparing to battle for football's biggest prize in the world. This is a clash that brings together two generations of greatness, with Lionel Messi looking to add another unforgettable chapter to

item.title: Spain, Argentina Brace For World Cup Final
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Sunday's showpiece is likely to be the final World Cup appearance for Messi, widely considered the greatest player of all time. The post Spain, Argentina Brace For World Cup Final appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: IPPIS Fraud: Court orders final forfeiture of N941 million recovered from ghost workers
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has ordered the final forfeiture of the sum of N941,994,079.86 linked to suspected ghost workers uncovered during the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission's (ICPC) investigation into the Integrated Payroll and Personnel Information Syste

item.title: Sudan: The War the World Stopped Watching
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] There is an old saying in journalism: when the cameras leave, the suffering remains.

item.title: 🔴 Middle East live: Iran's Guards say two ships involved in 'accident' after defying Hormuz warnings
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Step-By-Step Guide On How To Register For Your PVC On A Weekend
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: As the 2027 presidential elections draw closer, the deadline (July 26th) for registering people who need to get their PVCs draws closer. The PVC is the personal voter’s card that allows Nigerian citizens over 18 to vote for their candidate of choice in Nigerian elections. If you haven’t registered f

item.title: New York mayor: We’re considering arresting Netanyahu at UNGA
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: New York City Mayor, Zohran Mamdani, has said his administration is reviewing whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu could be arrested if he travels to New York for the 81st United Nations General Assembly (UNGA). World leaders are expected to attend the annual UNGA forum, which will hold

item.title: 'Legend' or 'regrets'? A final that will define Lamine Yamal's World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Lamine Yamal may not have had a sparkling World Cup but victory for Spain in Sunday's World Cup final would write a very different narrative.

item.title: Should Africa’s Giant Join the Global AI Governance Revolution?
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Artificial Intelligence is no longer merely a technological curiosity. It has become the new currency of economic power, geopolitical influence, national security, scientific advancement and human development. The post Should Africa’s Giant Join the Global AI Governance Revolution? appeared fi

item.title: Gaza cheers for Spain as World Cup final anticipation mounts
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Football fans in Gaza express support for Spain in showdown with Argentina, citing Spanish solidarity with Palestinians.

item.title: Fatosa pledges innovation hubs in seven Kogi West LGAs
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) senatorial candidate for Kogi West, Muyi Fatosa, has pledged to establish Innovation and Creative... The post Fatosa pledges innovation hubs in seven Kogi West LGAs appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Osun Guber: Tinubu has not abandoned Bola Oyebamiji — Oyintiloye
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A chieftain of the All Progressives Congress, APC, in Osun State, Olatunbosun Oyintiloye, has reaffirmed that President Bola Tinubu is behind Bola Oyebamiji, the party’s candidate in the August 15, 2026 governorship election. Oyintiloye spoke on Sunday in Ibokun, Obokun Loc

item.title: Troops Dismantle Kidnap Syndicate, Recover Ransom in Imo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of the 34 Artillery Brigade operating under Operation UDO KA, in collaboration with other security agencies, have dismantled a kidnapping syndicate in Imo State, arresting a suspect and recovering suspected ransom proceeds and a vehicle allegedly used in an abduction. The operation, conducted
